name: Band Lateral Raise
resistance_profile: Elastic tension rises as the band stretches, so it is easiest at the bottom and hardest near the top (shortened / peak-contraction) — the opposite of a cable's near-constant pull.
cues: Lead with the elbows, not the hands
Keep the traps down as you raise
Fight the band on the way down
execution_steps: Stand on the middle of a light resistance band with feet hip-width apart, holding an end or handle in each hand at your sides.
Set a tall chest, soft knees, and a slight bend in the elbows so tension is on the band.
Lead with the elbows and raise both arms out to the sides until the hands reach roughly shoulder height.
Pause briefly at the top without shrugging the traps.
Lower under control against the band's pull, reset tension at the bottom, and repeat for reps.
good_when: Training at home or travelling with minimal equipment
You want a joint-friendly warm-up or a high-rep burnout for the side delts
bad_when: You want to load the stretched bottom position hard (a cable or lean-away raise is better)
You need precise, repeatable load increments to track progress
common_errors: Using a band so heavy that momentum takes over
Shrugging the shoulders to reach the top
Letting the band snap the arms back down
progressions: Move to a heavier or shorter band, or step wider to add tension
Add reps, then switch to cable lateral raises for a loaded stretch
regressions: Perform single-arm or shorten the range to a comfortable height
